pi_items.source: https://docs.openbci.com/Cyton/CytonDataFormat/ - Official OpenBCI Cyton documentation confirms accelerometer streams at 25Hz by default. Documentation states: 'We can still fit a 25Hz accelerometer in with time stamps due to some interlacing and timing constraints. Since we stream channel data at 250Hz and accelerometer at 25Hz; we have essentially 10 samples to send the accelerometer data in.'
Code analysis - Path is consistently referenced in setup_task.sh (line 19), export_result.sh (line 8), and task description (line 5). Path structure follows standard Linux home directory conventions.

pi_items.metadata_value: Implied by task description 'change it to 25Hz'
pi_items.source: https://docs.openbci.com/Cyton/CytonSDK/ and https://openbci.com/forum/index.php?p=/discussion/1633/hardware-settings-on-the-cyton - Official documentation indicates Hardware Settings for Cyton allows configuration of: PGA Gain, Bias, Input Type, SRB1, SRB2. No documentation found showing accelerometer sample rate is configurable. 25Hz appears to be a fixed hardware specification for Cyton board.

https://docs.openbci.com/Cyton/CytonDataFormat/ - Multiple official documentation sources confirm Cyton accelerometer operates at fixed 25Hz rate. Also confirmed in webfetch results from official Cyton Data Format documentation.

pi_items.metadata_value: Implied by task asking to configure accelerometer sample rate
pi_items.verified_value: Partial - Accelerometer can be toggled on/off and different modes exist, but sample rate configuration not documented
pi_items.source: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIDocs/ and https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI - Official GUI documentation mentions three modes (default with Accelerometer, Analog Mode, Digital Mode) but does not document accelerometer sample rate as a configurable Hardware Settings parameter.

privileged_info_summary: The Cyton accelerometer sample rate is 25Hz, which is verified as the standard fixed specification for OpenBCI Cyton boards according to official documentation. The expected screenshot path is properly configured and consistent throughout task scripts. However, the accelerometer sample rate appears to be a FIXED hardware specification rather than a configurable parameter in the OpenBCI GUI Hardware Settings panel, despite the task description implying it should be 'changed' to 25Hz. The Hardware Settings panel allows configuration of channel parameters (gain, bias, input type, reference settings) but not accelerometer sample rate configuration per official documentation. This suggests either: (1) the task uses a synthetic/modified GUI with additional features not present in standard OpenBCI, or (2) there is a contradiction between the task requirement and actual OpenBCI GUI capabilities.
